accepted_runtime_boundaries.completion_rule: Accepted runtime boundaries support full Python generated CLI completion only when every generated facade or generated operation function-call package-domain source call is listed as an exact source module and symbol, mechanically checked against generated call sites, and declared as a permanent package-domain boundary rather than generic deterministic runtime debt.

entries.generic_behavior_audit: Classified as package-specific-judgment; generated parser and command dispatch own the command projection while the package runtime boundary owns ignored-local session log containment, pointer validation, append/write policy, and compatibility output.
entries.minimization_owner: package runtime primitive owner for semantic package judgment
entries.stale_when: Stale when session log status, note append, and new-session behavior can be represented as stable declarative dataflow with equivalent ignored-local containment and compatibility safeguards.
entries.why_not_generic_deterministic: The symbol owns ignored-local session log path containment, pointer validation, append/write policy, and compatibility output rather than generic deterministic target-executor logic.

entries.generic_behavior_audit: #1658 group: mutation scaffolding with package-owned transaction safety. Deterministic dry-run plan, target/resource, and compact output mechanics are extraction candidates, but semantic owner after split is Memory lifecycle/note mutation safety and provenance. Further movement waits for command-generation#75.
entries.minimization_owner: package runtime primitive owner for mutation, safety, conflict, and provenance policy
entries.stale_when: Stale when command-generation#75 can render/prove generic transaction scaffolding while keeping Memory mutation safety and provenance hooks explicit.
entries.why_not_generic_deterministic: The symbol owns package lifecycle or planning-state mutation policy, validation, conflict handling, or provenance updates that are not portable target-executor behavior.

entries.generic_behavior_audit: #1658 group: mutation scaffolding with package-owned transaction safety. entries.why_not_generic_deterministic: The symbol owns package lifecycle or planning-state mutation policy, validation, conflict handling, or provenance updates that are not portable target-executor behavior.

entries.generic_behavior_audit: #1658 group: durable-knowledge semantic judgment. Route, search, capture, review, or sync behavior owns relevance, durability, route quality, and capture-vs-route judgment. Semantic owner after split remains Memory; this must not move into command-generation as keyword/prose authority.
entries.minimization_owner: package runtime primitive owner for semantic package judgment
entries.stale_when: Stale only when a real semantic boundary exists that preserves Memory relevance, durability, route-quality, and capture-vs-route judgment outside generic code generation.
entries.why_not_generic_deterministic: The symbol owns package-specific policy, compatibility, or payload judgment behind an explicit operation primitive boundary rather than generic deterministic target-executor logic.

entries.generic_behavior_audit: #1658 group: view/payload scaffolding. Compact action/result shaping, tiny payloads, prompt/template selection, or report payload mechanics are extraction candidates; semantic owner after split is Memory view, prompt, route-report, current-memory, or promotion policy. Shared compact action shaping is now isolated in runtime_primitives. Further movement waits for command-generation#74.
entries.minimization_owner: package runtime primitive owner for semantic package judgment
entries.stale_when: Stale when command-generation#74 can render/prove declared Memory view/resource/payload scaffolding without absorbing Memory semantic judgment.
entries.why_not_generic_deterministic: The symbol owns Memory promotion-report compact JSON compatibility and package-specific payload judgment behind an explicit operation primitive boundary rather than generic deterministic target-executor logic.

entries.generic_behavior_audit: #1657 group: conditional dispatch plus value-map/default/coercion. Planning now centralizes each branch mapping through _call_planning_operation, but the parent-lane-closeout branch selection remains hand-owned until command-generation#73, and branch mutation semantics remain package-owned. Value mapping also waits for command-generation#72.
entries.minimization_owner: package runtime primitive owner for mutation, safety, conflict, and provenance policy
entries.stale_when: Stale when command-generation#73 can render and prove declared conditional dispatch and command-generation#72 can render branch value/default/coercion mappings without absorbing Planning mutation semantics.
entries.why_not_generic_deterministic: The symbol owns package lifecycle or planning-state mutation policy, validation, conflict handling, or provenance updates that are not portable target-executor behavior.

entries.generic_behavior_audit: #1657 group: value-map plus defaults/coercions. Planning now declares this portable layer through _call_planning_operation/_planning_operation_value with focused tests; the package semantic function still owns mutation, validation, safety, and provenance. Further movement waits for command-generation#72.
entries.minimization_owner: package runtime primitive owner for mutation, safety, conflict, and provenance policy
entries.stale_when: Stale when command-generation#72 can render and prove the declared value/default/coercion call adapter while leaving the package semantic primitive explicit.

entries.generic_behavior_audit: generated-owned output coverage: JSON dict emission, selected-output text, compact-answer text, delegation-outcomes text, memory route/report text, planning-module tiny text, and config.report JSON/tiny/compact/full/selected text is handled by generated output.emit text_views before source fallback; planning-module tiny text and config.report output.emit text_views are now owned by codegen output primitives. declared view coverage: defaults-router text consumes defaults-router.text from src/agentic_workspace/contracts/workspace_output_views.json. accepted source fallback: _emit_workspace_prompt_output, _emit_workspace_system_intent_output, _emit_workspace_delegation_outcomes_output, _emit_workspace_compact_section_answer_output, and _emit_workspace_defaults_compat_output name the remaining package-specific output judgment because their wording is coupled to workspace policy and compatibility contracts. not accepted as generic output: generic JSON/text dict emission is already generated-owned and this exact symbol is accepted only as the dispatcher for the named package-specific fallback cases.
entries.minimization_owner: package runtime primitive owner for semantic package judgment
entries.stale_when: Stale when the named workspace output policy helpers are replaced by declared view specs plus a portable CG view/render primitive, or when additional generic output behavior is added to this dispatcher without an inventory update.
entries.why_not_generic_deterministic: Generated JSON, selected-output, compact-answer, delegation-outcome, memory route/report text, and codegen-owned planning-module tiny text are emitted before source fallback. Defaults-router text now consumes declared view metadata from src/agentic_workspace/contracts/workspace_output_views.json. The accepted source fallback is now split into named workspace policy helpers for the remaining package-specific output judgment: prompt payload text, workspace-system-intent text, delegation-outcome compatibility, compact section answers, and defaults compatibility surfaces whose wording depends on workspace package policy.

completion_rule: The Python CLI completion gate reaches full-generated-cli-complete only when generated Python projections are rendered by command-generation, direct generated command modules avoid package runtime imports, generated facades and operation function-call steps reach package-domain runtime only through exact accepted source-symbol boundaries, and every accepted boundary names its generated caller, primitive refs, owner, conformance, permanence, and generic-behavior audit.

representative_migrations.fail_closed_rule: The checker must fail when the generated command module, generated operation contract, command-package IR adapter, or accepted runtime boundary no longer matches this representative migration.

remaining_runtime_boundary.why_runtime_owned: Planning closeout still owns package-specific mutation safety, proof-to-claim reconciliation, archive decisions, and continuation/residue updates behind the generated operation primitive.

representative_migrations.fail_closed_rule: The checker must fail when the generated config command module, generated config.report operation resources, command-package IR adapter, declared output.emit text views, or accepted workspace config runtime boundary no longer matches this representative migration.

remaining_runtime_boundary.why_runtime_owned: The root config command still owns package-specific config layering, local override state, module descriptor validation, and payload assembly behind the generated config.report operation primitive; selected-output projection, generated callable conformance, and config JSON/tiny/compact/full/selected output emission are IR/codegen-owned through output.emit text_views.

ordinary_command_migration.rule: Ordinary command behavior should be represented by command-package IR and generated command/operation artifacts; package runtime remains only for named primitive boundaries with exact source-symbol ownership.

purpose: Track generated/python runtime and operation-executor Python files whose location alone could be mistaken for generated ownership.
